description: Complete biaxial (or triaxial) compression test workflow for PFC 5.0, from model setup through specimen preparation, consolidation, and shearing with post-peak detection. Includes full working FISH functions.

sections.heading: Workflow Overview
sections.content:
A standard biaxial compression test in PFC 5.0 follows these phases:

  Phase 1 — Model Setup
    - new (clear model)
    - domain extent (set boundaries)
    - cmat default (set contact model)
    - wall generate (create 6 walls)

  Phase 2 — Specimen Generation
    - set random (seed for reproducibility)
    - ball distribute (generate particles)
    - ball attribute (set density, damping)

  Phase 3 — Initial Equilibrium
    - cycle calm (dissipate energy)
    - solve aratio (convergence check)

  Phase 4 — Consolidation (isotropic or K0)
    - FISH servo for confining pressure
    - cycle to equilibrium at target stress

  Phase 5 — Shearing
    - Assign constant velocity to top/bottom walls
    - Track stress/strain via FISH history
    - Use solve fishhalt for post-peak detection

  Phase 6 — Data Output
    - History write to file
    - Plot generation (stress-strain curve, etc.)

sections.heading: Phase 1 — Model Setup Commands
sections.content:
new
  domain extent -0.05 0.05 -0.05 0.05 -0.08 0.08 condition destroy

  ; Contact model: linear (no bonding for soil)
  cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1.0e8 kratio 1.0
  cmat default type ball-wall model linear property kn 1.0e9 ks 1.0e9 fric 0.0

  ; Create 6 walls (IDs 1-6)
  wall generate id 1 plane dip 90 dd 90 position -0.025 0 0   ; x-min
  wall generate id 2 plane dip 90 dd 90 position 0.025 0 0    ; x-max
  wall generate id 3 plane dip 90 dd 0 position 0 -0.025 0    ; y-min
  wall generate id 4 plane dip 90 dd 0 position 0 0.025 0     ; y-max
  wall generate id 5 plane dip 0 dd 0 position 0 0 -0.04      ; z-min (bottom)
  wall generate id 6 plane dip 0 dd 0 position 0 0 0.04       ; z-max (top)

Note: Adjust wall positions to match desired sample dimensions. Wall IDs 1-4 are confining walls, 5-6 are loading platens.

sections.heading: Phase 2 — Specimen Generation
sections.content:
set random 10001

  ; Generate particles with target porosity
  ball distribute porosity 0.15 radius 1.0e-3 1.8e-3 \
    box -0.024 0.024 -0.024 0.024 -0.039 0.039

  ; Set particle properties
  ball attribute density 2700.0
  ball attribute damp 0.7
  ball attribute xvelocity 0.0
  ball attribute yvelocity 0.0
  ball attribute zvelocity 0.0
  ball attribute spin 0.0

Note: The generation box should be slightly smaller than the wall boundaries to avoid particles being created overlapping walls. Use the 'box' extent within the wall extent.

sections.heading: Phase 3 — Initial Equilibrium
sections.content:
; Cycle with calming to dissipate kinetic energy
  cycle 1000 calm 50

  ; Solve to force equilibrium (average ratio < 1e-4)
  solve aratio 1.0e-4

  ; Measure initial sample dimensions
  define get_sample_extents
    local xmin = 1e12; local xmax = -1e12
    local ymin = 1e12; local ymax = -1e12
    local zmin = 1e12; local zmax = -1e12
    loop foreach bp ball.list
      local r = ball.radius(bp)
      xmin = math.min(xmin, ball.pos.x(bp) - r)
      xmax = math.max(xmax, ball.pos.x(bp) + r)
      ymin = math.min(ymin, ball.pos.y(bp) - r)
      ymax = math.max(ymax, ball.pos.y(bp) + r)
      zmin = math.min(zmin, ball.pos.z(bp) - r)
      zmax = math.max(zmax, ball.pos.z(bp) + r)
    end_loop
    sample_width = xmax - xmin
    sample_depth = ymax - ymin
    sample_height = zmax - zmin
  end
  @get_sample_extents

  ; Compute initial cross-sectional area
  if global.dim = 2 then
    cross_area = sample_width  ; unit thickness in 2D
  else
    ; For 3D: rectangular cross-section in x-y plane
    cross_area = sample_width * sample_depth
  end_if

  initial_height = sample_height

sections.heading: Phase 4 — Consolidation (Confining Pressure Servo)
sections.content:
; Confining stress targets
  conf_stress = 1.0e5  ; 100 kPa confining pressure
  gain = 0.5
  max_vel = 0.005

  ; Wall IDs for confining walls
  wall_xmin = wall.find(1)
  wall_xmax = wall.find(2)
  wall_ymin = wall.find(3)
  wall_ymax = wall.find(4)

  ; Each axis needs its own servo because wall areas differ
  define servo_x
    while_stepping
    local force1 = wall.force.contact(wall_xmin, 1)
    local force2 = wall.force.contact(wall_xmax, 1)
    local area = sample_height * sample_depth
    local avg_stress = (force1 + force2) / (2.0 * area)
    local err = conf_stress - avg_stress
    local vel = gain * err
    if math.abs(vel) > max_vel then
      vel = max_vel * (vel / math.abs(vel))
    end_if
    command
      wall attribute xvelocity [vel] range id 1
      wall attribute xvelocity [-vel] range id 2
    end_command
  end

  define servo_y
    while_stepping
    local force1 = wall.force.contact(wall_ymin, 2)
    local force2 = wall.force.contact(wall_ymax, 2)
    local area = sample_height * sample_width
    local avg_stress = (force1 + force2) / (2.0 * area)
    local err = conf_stress - avg_stress
    local vel = gain * err
    if math.abs(vel) > max_vel then
      vel = max_vel * (vel / math.abs(vel))
    end_if
    command
      wall attribute yvelocity [vel] range id 3
      wall attribute yvelocity [-vel] range id 4
    end_command
  end

  ; Also fix the top/bottom platens during consolidation
  command
    wall attribute zvelocity 0.0 range id 5
    wall attribute zvelocity 0.0 range id 6
  end_command

  ; Activate servos and cycle to equilibrium
  cycle 2000 calm 50
  solve aratio 1.0e-4

sections.heading: Phase 5 — Shearing (Loading)
sections.content:
; Deactivate confining servos (or keep them for constant confining stress)
  ; For constant lateral stress, keep servo_x and servo_y running.
  ; For zero lateral strain (K0), set wall velocities to 0 and reactivate servos.

  ; Axial loading velocity (strain rate control)
  axial_rate = 0.1  ; strain rate (e.g., 10% per second in model time)
  load_vel = axial_rate * initial_height / 2.0  ; velocity per platen

  ; Stress/strain tracking
  define axial_stress_fn
    local force_top = wall.force.contact(wall_top, 3)
    local force_bot = wall.force.contact(wall_bot, 3)
    local avg_force = (force_top + force_bot) / 2.0
    axial_stress_fn = avg_force / cross_area
  end

  define axial_strain_fn
    local top_disp = wall.disp(wall_top, 3)
    local bot_disp = wall.disp(wall_bot, 3)
    axial_strain_fn = -(top_disp - bot_disp) / initial_height
  end

  ; Store wall pointers for use in functions
  wall_top = wall.find(6)
  wall_bot = wall.find(5)

  ; Apply constant velocity to platens
  command
    wall attribute zvelocity [load_vel] range id 6
    wall attribute zvelocity [-load_vel] range id 5
  end_command

  ; Set up histories
  history id 1 @axial_stress_fn
  history id 2 @axial_strain_fn
  history id 3 mechanical solve unbalanced

  ; Shearing with solve (strain-controlled)
  target_strain = 0.2  ; 20% axial strain
  target_disp = target_strain * initial_height / 2.0

  solve time 0.1  ; or use cycle N for fixed step count

sections.heading: Post-Peak Detection with solve fishhalt
sections.content:
For detecting peak stress and post-peak softening, use 'solve fishhalt':

  ; Track peak stress
  peak_stress = 0.0

  define check_peak
    if axial_stress_fn > peak_stress then
      peak_stress = axial_stress_fn
    end_if
    ; Halt if stress drops 20% below peak (post-peak detection)
    if peak_stress > 0 then
      if axial_stress_fn / peak_stress < 0.8 then
        check_peak = 1  ; halts the solve
      end_if
    end_if
  end

  ; Load until post-peak detected or max strain reached
  command
    solve fishhalt @check_peak
  end_command

  ; Alternative: halt at a specific strain level
  define halt_at_strain
    if axial_strain_fn > 0.15 then
      halt_at_strain = 1
    end_if
  end

  command
    solve fishhalt @halt_at_strain
  end_command

Note: 'solve fishhalt' checks the named function each step. If it returns non-zero (true), the solve halts. The function must exist and return a value.

sections.heading: Complete Initialization FISH Functions
sections.content:
Consolidated initialization block for a PFC 5.0 biaxial test:

  define initialize
    ; Set cross-sectional area and height for stress/strain
    local xmin = 1e12; local xmax = -1e12
    local ymin = 1e12; local ymax = -1e12
    local zmin = 1e12; local zmax = -1e12
    loop foreach bp ball.list
      local r = ball.radius(bp)
      xmin = math.min(xmin, ball.pos.x(bp) - r)
      xmax = math.max(xmax, ball.pos.x(bp) + r)
      ymin = math.min(ymin, ball.pos.y(bp) - r)
      ymax = math.max(ymax, ball.pos.y(bp) + r)
      zmin = math.min(zmin, ball.pos.z(bp) - r)
      zmax = math.max(zmax, ball.pos.z(bp) + r)
    end_loop
    sample_height = zmax - zmin
    sample_width  = xmax - xmin
    sample_depth  = ymax - ymin
    if global.dim = 2 then
      cross_area = sample_width * 1.0 ; unit thickness
    else
      cross_area = sample_width * sample_depth
    end_if
    initial_height = sample_height

    ; Store wall pointers for axial loading
    wall_top = wall.find(6)
    wall_bot = wall.find(5)

    ; Initial servo go-to positions (if using target stress)
    io.out('Initial height: ' + string(initial_height))
    io.out('Cross area: ' + string(cross_area))
  end

sections.heading: Complete Axial Stress/Strain Functions
sections.content:
These functions work with the initialization block above:

  define axial_stress
    local wp_top = wall_top
    local wp_bot = wall_bot
    if wp_top = null or wp_bot = null then
      axial_stress = 0.0
      exit
    end_if
    local f_top = wall.force.contact(wp_top, 3)
    local f_bot = wall.force.contact(wp_bot, 3)
    local f_avg = (f_top + f_bot) / 2.0
    axial_stress = f_avg / cross_area
  end

  define axial_strain
    if wall_top = null then
      axial_strain = 0.0
      exit
    end_if
    local disp_top = wall.disp(wall_top, 3)
    local disp_bot = wall.disp(wall_bot, 3)
    local net_strain = (disp_top - disp_bot) / initial_height
    axial_strain = -net_strain  ; positive in compression
  end

  define update_area
    while_stepping
    ; Recompute area if sample deforms > 5%
    if axial_strain > 0.05 then
      local xmin = 1e12; local xmax = -1e12
      local ymin = 1e12; local ymax = -1e12
      loop foreach bp ball.list
        local r = ball.radius(bp)
        xmin = math.min(xmin, ball.pos(bp, 1) - r)
        xmax = math.max(xmax, ball.pos(bp, 1) + r)
        ymin = math.min(ymin, ball.pos(bp, 2) - r)
        ymax = math.max(ymax, ball.pos(bp, 2) + r)
      end_loop
      cross_area = (xmax - xmin) * (ymax - ymin)
    end_if
  end

sections.heading: Practical Notes
sections.content:
- Calm cycling: 'cycle N calm 50' zeros ball velocities every 50 steps, helping energy dissipation during initial packing. Increase frequency (calm 10) for unstable packings.
- Solve aratio: 'solve aratio 1e-4' stops when max unbalanced force / avg contact force < 1e-4. This is the standard equilibrium criterion.
- Servo gain tuning: Start with gain = 0.5 and max_vel = 0.005. If stress oscillates, reduce gain. If response is too slow, increase gain.
- Strain rate: Use axial_rate = 0.01 to 0.1 s^-1 for quasi-static conditions. Higher rates introduce dynamic effects. Lower rates (0.001) are more quasi-static but require more steps.
- Post-peak: Use 'solve fishhalt' to detect peak and stop after softening. Without this, the simulation continues well past peak, producing unrealistic deformations.
- Domain extent: Set generously, especially with 'condition destroy' for safety. Balls that exit the domain are deleted, preventing numerical issues.
- Datafiles: Always write histories to files. Use 'history write id all file "biaxial_results.his"' after the solve for post-processing.
